# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Mushihimesama
- Steam AppID of the game: 377860

## System Information
Steam Deck LCD
SteamOS 3.7.9
- Proton version: 9.0

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
(Proton logs compress well if needed.)-->

## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->

On Proton 9.0, after launching the game at least once, subsequent launches of the game on the Steam Deck require pressing a button for the game to actually start (I think it's the start button you have to press) or else it sits at a black screen.

On Proton 7.0 I noticed I didn't have this issue, the game boots up without pressing the start button on the Steam Deck and doesn't hang on a blackscreen until the button is pressed.

## Reproduction

Start Mushihimesama with Proton 9.0
Play the game for a bit, then quit (i don't remember if this step is required)
Start the game up again.
Notice black screen.
Press the start button on the Steam Deck.
Notice the game finally boots up.

Play the game in Proton 7.0
Notice you don't have the above issue

@pizzadude I can reproduce this partially, start button doesn't get the blackscreen go away, but connecting a keyboard and typing z does. Also found that using the volume/steam menu buttons makes the blackscreen go away and also makes it not appear in subsequent launches (seems to require wiping the proton prefix and rebooting for it to come back).

I also haven't gotten the blackscreen issue when using proton 10, have you tried using that version? And if so, have you had the blackscreen issue there?
It might also help to post a proton log too, to help out, i can check and post one later today if needed
This bug is very inconsistent, I can't reproduce it all the time, which is weird, maybe has to do with swapping proton versions.

@pizzadude from what I see, interacting with the steam ui while the game is open seems to make the issue inconsistent to reproduce
@waspennator no matter how many times I try, I haven't been able to get this blackscreen issue with proton 10 or experimental. Wiping the prefix and rebooting haven't helped the issue manifest. For me, when I encountered the issue, the start button wouldn't help, but typing z from an external keyboard made it work for that launch, while volume and steam buttons would also make it work and also seemingly prevent the issue from occurring in subsequent launches

following_discussion.discussion_body: Hello @PorcelainMouse, looking more closely at your issue report, you're using Feral Interactive's native linux port of the game. Proton is a compatibility layer to run Windows based games on Linux, and since you're using the native linux variant of the game, this project isn't involved at all with your issue.

Thanks for looking at it! You know, that's what I thought, but I figured I didn't understand proton well enough to be sure.  I was just following Steam's support instructions.  I guess I'm dead in the water.  Their support wasn't able to fix it, either.

Don't know about desktop PCs, but on my notebook {5800H & RTX3070} DLSS is not working, I tried blacklist AMD iGPU, update nvapi and nvngx_dlss, insert '__NV_PRIME_RENDER_OFFLOAD=1 __GLX_VENDOR_LIBRARY_NAME=nvidia PROTON_ENABLE_NVAPI=1  PROTON_HIDE_NVIDIA_GPU=0 %command%'. Log always report:
`DLSS support test, code = 0x00000001, info: NVSDK_NGX_Result_Success`
`Disabling DLSS as it is not supported on non Nvidia cards`

I insert **DXVK_CONFIG_FILE=/home/wer/.local/dxvk.conf DXVK_FILTER_DEVICE_NAME="NVIDIA GeForce RTX 3070 Laptop" PROTON_ENABLE_NVAPI=1 %command%** to game settings window.
 **dxvk.conf** contain only:
`dxgi.nvapiHack = False`
And voila DLSS working now.
Its strange, but DLSS don't provide additional performance, 100fps w/o any AA and 104 with DLSS quality, also GPU heavily underclocked, sometimes only 55-65% load.

`PROTON_ENABLE_NVAPI=1 PROTON_HIDE_NVIDIA_GPU=0 DXVK_CONFIG_FILE=(path-to-file)` with `dxgi.nvapiHack = False` is enough to get DLSS to work fine for me.

The game does still stutter pretty hard from time to time though, hanging for few seconds on a single frame, but this goes away over time the more the game is played. Though, the framerate can decide to swing wildly from time-to-time depending on the resolution set.

![Screenshot_20220724_101906](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/98650216/180637105-0ed2d0f6-b5a3-496f-b61b-7480607ea2db.png)

Bug that prevents me from progressing in the game.
On the other side there should be a bucket where you can attach the rope.
I don't know what to do to fix this.

This is a little weird to explain so bare with me here.

i'm using a Dualsense joystick and on windows the game detects it as a DS4 if steam input is disabled

on linux, first of all i changed my dualsense light bar color to steam purple and whenever steam input is on that will be the light bar color, otherwise it will be blue (games that support dualsense natively can behave differently, anyways).

so lately on linux only, by having playstation controller support setting enabled, even if steam input is disable i can tell its there somehow because the light bar is purple, even if the virtual xbox controller that steam input creates is not there its still somehow on, its just no passing any inputs...

and that seems to be interfering in this game in particular because on windows i could have dualshock 4 prompt buttons if steam input was disabled, here its no possible at all because steam input is somehow interfering, and totally disabling playstation controller support simply makes it invisible to the game...

the linux native version of the game does have generic support for all playstation controllers but for the dualsense it enumerates its properties incorrectly and the motion sensors part keeps getting in the way and the only workaround i know is to disable the motion sensor part of the controller on a system level so the game doesn't enumerate it.

this could also be fixed if steam input was also able to expose the dualsense controller and its similar capabilities as a dualshock 4 instead of a generic xbox controller...

I think I found the reason for the game crashing when it is building shaders is that it's trying to load a shader that it does not support, and that shader is called "nir_intrinsic_trace_ray_intel". This happens in some Unreal Engine games, one being the finals, where at first it couldn't get past the shader loading, but after the game updated, I was able to load shaders just fine. Marvel's rivals, after the game update, I am still unable to fully load shaders. Other games, not built on Unreal Engine, are experiencing these issues that I was unaware of. I think that's the issue, but someone was able to get around it somehow on the Mesa GitLab website.

Once this is "fixed", the launcher displays the error "**This game requires Windows 10**" ![unsupported_os](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1810484/113131541-a2ebc780-921d-11eb-9bbf-85c97c679c78.png) What I believe is happening is that the structure of the kernel32.dll file is inspected to determine its version number. Unfortunately for us, the default version chosen in Wine is the Windows 7 version \[[source](https://github.com/wine-mirror/wine/blob/master/dlls/kernel32/version.rc)\] Note: Changing the version in winecfg has no effects
Wine sources (click to expand)

## Wine sources --- #define WINE_FILEVERSION_STR "6.1.7601.17514" #define WINE_PRODUCTVERSION_STR "6.1.7601.17514" ---

The cleanest workaround would be to download the Proton sources, update the version and recompile, but it is quite a tedious task especially as we need only one file. The quickest I have found is to achieve the same result by directly editing the **kernel32.dll** (ProtonGE) and **kernel32.dll.so** (Proton) files.*(ps: Make sure PROTON\_WINEROOT exists for your system)*
Linux sed commands (click to expand)

## Linux sed commands --- export PROTON_WINEROOT="/home/${USER}/.local/share/Steam/steamapps/common/Proton 5.13/dist/lib64/wine/" # For Proton GE sed -i.bak 's/\x01\x00\x06\x00\x6a\x44\xb1\x1d/\x00\x00\x0a\x00\x6a\x44\xb1\x1d/g' "${PROTON_WINEROOT}/kernel32.dll" > /dev/null 2>&1 # For Proton sed -i.bak 's/\x01\x00\x06\x00\x6a\x44\xb1\x1d/\x00\x00\x0a\x00\x6a\x44\xb1\x1d/g' "${PROTON_WINEROOT}/kernel32.dll.so" > /dev/null 2>&1 ---

This will result in saving the original file under the .bak extension, and will produce a new file as if it had been compiled with the following values
Wine's new configuration (click to expand)

## Linux sed commands --- #define WINE_FILEVERSION_STR "10.0.7601.17514" #define WINE_PRODUCTVERSION_STR "10.0.7601.17514" ---

The launcher is then launched correctly, everything seem to work, to the exception, the background is white and the launch window cannot be moved. ![evilgenius2_launcher](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1810484/113132958-7173fb80-921f-11eb-97cc-6f03330cbd62.png) ![evilgenius2_settings](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1810484/113133958-8ef59500-9220-11eb-9221-778ce8c78c9a.png) After testing a little bit further I'm assuming it is coming from a missing interface in Windows Imaging Component windowscodecs.dll, the game launcher is probably linked against a Windows 10 version, in other words, the windowscodecs.dll we have available with winetricks is too old to display the launcher as crystal clear as on Windows 10.

Okay I finally found the solution and the reason why Elden Ring was not launching for me anymore! The short answer is that for some reason you have to trigger Steam to reinstall the dependencies like EAC and so on in the prefix. Something during one of the last updates of the game simply broke compatibility with existing installations. I don't know any details beyond these assumptions, but here is how to fix it:

As I found on ProtonDB, someone suggested to "Backup and delete the Elden Ring (1245620) subdirectory in compatdata".

1. Backup and delete the Elden Ring (1245620) subdirectory in compatdata -> Simply cut and paste it into another folder (e.g. ~/Downloads/). I want to reiterate that this step of essentially backing up this directory is very important! **Your saves are stored in there!**
2. Start Elden Ring via Steam. It will now create a fresh prefix folder (1245620 in Steam's compatdata folder). It will also install all dependencies like EAC, etc.
3. Now you have essentially a fresh install of Elden Ring with no save games. The game will already launch fine. Now we just need to restore your saves from the backed up folder!
4. Your backed up folder (that you pasted somewhere else) is called "1245620". Now navigate to "1245620/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/AppData/Roaming/EldenRing/" and navigate to this same location in the "1245620" folder that was just re-created by Steam in the compatdata folder. After this step, you should have both "EldenRing" folders open side by side. From your backed up folder, simply copy the folder containing your save games as well as the "GraphicsConfig.xml" into the other corresponding folder used by Steam. Now you have all of your save games as they were before.
5. Start the game and you will be able to continue from your saves as expected. You are now good to go! Enjoy!

Elden Ring stops handling input from the Steam Deck's integrated controller on the SteamOS 3.5 from the main branch and the latest Steam beta client. An external xinput gamepad connected via bluetooth continues to work once the game gets into this bad state.

The controller doesn't appear to have its firmware in a bad state either. It's fully functional in the OS. The d-pad, the ABXY buttons, triggers, STEAM button, QAM button, other buttons and the joysticks are all working properly in the OS.

Getting it out of this state involves force closing the game and starting it again. It doesn't take down the OS with it in this failure mode. The dmesg output doesn't show anything particularly interesting. It has messages related to LUTs and color profile (debug messages containing "Josh color"). Perhaps these were part of the ongoing work with color profiles.

The game also responds to keyboard input, touch screen input and touchpad as mouse input while it is in the bad state.

I've been able to reproduce the issue with another device running Windows with an xinput controller. I had to close the game and start it again on that other device. The other device was on the stable version of Steam.

The fact that two different devices running different operating systems with different hardware had the same issue rules out a few potential culprits: Proton, SteamOS, my Steam Deck, the controllers themselves and Windows. The game was also not updated recently. That leaves us only with a bug in Steam input.

As an additional bonus, Steam input didn't set up my xinput controller properly. Is there some broken profile applied incorrectly? It's odd because it used to work just fine. It always has.
